item.title: IRAN CLAIMS IT HAS HIGH-SPEED UNDERWATER-LAUNCHED MISSILES: CNN
item.description: IRAN CLAIMS IT HAS HIGH-SPEED UNDERWATER-LAUNCHED MISSILES: CNN

item.title:
🚨 IRAN CLAIMS UNDERWATER MISSILES, THREATENS STRAIT OF HORMUZ
Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps says it has high-speed missiles launched from underwater, capable of 100 meters per second. Commander Ali Fadavi claimed only Iran and Russia have this tech and suggested they might be used soon. Analysts see the statements as deterrence signaling, highlighting Tehran’s naval capabilities in the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil and gas route. CNN has not verified the claims.
item.description:
🚨 IRAN CLAIMS UNDERWATER MISSILES, THREATENS STRAIT OF HORMUZ

Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps says it has high-speed missiles launched from underwater, capable of 100 meters per second. Commander Ali Fadavi claimed only Iran and Russia have this tech and suggested they might be used soon. Analysts see the statements as deterrence signaling, highlighting Tehran’s naval capabilities in the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil and gas route. CNN has not verified the claims.

item.title: Iran has deployed about a dozen mines in the Strait of Hormuz, halting oil and LNG exports and pushing energy prices higher. The U.S. Navy has destroyed 16 mine-laying vessels but is not escorting commercial ships. President Trump demanded immediate removal of the mines, warning of military consequences if Iran fails to comply. Iran’s military says oil could reach $200 a barrel.
item.description: Iran has deployed about a dozen mines in the Strait of Hormuz, halting oil and LNG exports and pushing energy prices higher. The U.S. Navy has destroyed 16 mine-laying vessels but is not escorting commercial ships. President Trump demanded immediate removal of the mines, warning of military consequences if Iran fails to comply. Iran’s military says oil could reach $200 a barrel.

item.title:
BLS STRUGGLES WITH DATA COLLECTION AMID STAFF SHORTAGE
The Bureau of Labor Statistics has faced staffing shortages and a federal hiring freeze, forcing reliance on less precise estimation methods for price data. In February, “different-cell imputation” was used in 40% of estimates, up from 9% a year ago. Relief may be coming as the BLS posts job openings for price-data collectors nationwide.
item.description:
BLS STRUGGLES WITH DATA COLLECTION AMID STAFF SHORTAGE

The Bureau of Labor Statistics has faced staffing shortages and a federal hiring freeze, forcing reliance on less precise estimation methods for price data. In February, “different-cell imputation” was used in 40% of estimates, up from 9% a year ago. Relief may be coming as the BLS posts job openings for price-data collectors nationwide.

item.title:
GERMAN FM: STRAIT OF HORMUZ BLOCKED, DIPLOMACY ONLY OPTION
German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul says the Strait of Hormuz is currently unnavigable, with no shipping movement and no protection available. He warns the blockage threatens energy security for Asia and Europe and says only diplomacy, not military action, can restore safe passage.
item.description:
GERMAN FM: STRAIT OF HORMUZ BLOCKED, DIPLOMACY ONLY OPTION

German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul says the Strait of Hormuz is currently unnavigable, with no shipping movement and no protection available. He warns the blockage threatens energy security for Asia and Europe and says only diplomacy, not military action, can restore safe passage.

item.title: 🚨 U.S. CENTRAL COMMAND WARNS CIVILIANS IN IRAN TO AVOID PORT FACILITIES WHERE IRANIAN NAVAL FORCES ARE OPERATING
item.description: 🚨 U.S. CENTRAL COMMAND WARNS CIVILIANS IN IRAN TO AVOID PORT FACILITIES WHERE IRANIAN NAVAL FORCES ARE OPERATING

item.title:
U.S. MILITARY MISTAKENLY STRIKES SCHOOL IN IRAN
A preliminary U.S. investigation finds the Feb. 28 Tomahawk missile strike on Shajarah Tayyebeh Elementary School in Minab was a targeting error. Outdated data from the Defense Intelligence Agency led Central Command to strike a building formerly part of a nearby naval base, killing at least 175 people, mostly children.
Officials say the error reflects human and procedural failures, not new technology. Satellite imagery and videos confirm U.S. responsibility. The investigation is ongoing, with questions about why data wasn’t verified. President Donald Trump initially blamed Iran but says he will accept the findings.
item.description:
U.S. MILITARY MISTAKENLY STRIKES SCHOOL IN IRAN

A preliminary U.S. investigation finds the Feb. 28 Tomahawk missile strike on Shajarah Tayyebeh Elementary School in Minab was a targeting error. Outdated data from the Defense Intelligence Agency led Central Command to strike a building formerly part of a nearby naval base, killing at least 175 people, mostly children.

Officials say the error reflects human and procedural failures, not new technology. Satellite imagery and videos confirm U.S. responsibility. The investigation is ongoing, with questions about why data wasn’t verified. President Donald Trump initially blamed Iran but says he will accept the findings.

item.title: Prison inmate charged with Huntley murder appears in court
item.description: Ian Huntley had been serving a life sentence with a minimum term of 40 years for the murders of 10-year-olds Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman.

item.title: Boy, 16, arrested after girl is stabbed at school
item.description: Sources tell the BBC that pupils had to hide under their desks as the incident unfolded.

item.title:
TRUMP CLAIMS WINNING IN IRAN BUT WAR’S END REMAINS UNCLEAR
President Donald Trump repeatedly says the U.S. is succeeding in Iran, but his objectives and exit plan remain vague, fueling domestic and international concern.
The 12-day conflict has shut the Strait of Hormuz, killed U.S. troops, and sparked Iranian retaliation, yet Trump alternates between claiming victory and threatening further action. U.S. allies, including Germany, stress the need for a clear, limited endgame, while polls show Americans are divided and skeptical of Trump’s decisions.
Criticism also grows over the girls’ school bombing, with Trump blaming Iran despite evidence of U.S. missiles hitting the site, raising questions about accountability and strategy.
item.description:
TRUMP CLAIMS WINNING IN IRAN BUT WAR’S END REMAINS UNCLEAR

President Donald Trump repeatedly says the U.S. is succeeding in Iran, but his objectives and exit plan remain vague, fueling domestic and international concern.

The 12-day conflict has shut the Strait of Hormuz, killed U.S. troops, and sparked Iranian retaliation, yet Trump alternates between claiming victory and threatening further action. U.S. allies, including Germany, stress the need for a clear, limited endgame, while polls show Americans are divided and skeptical of Trump’s decisions.

Criticism also grows over the girls’ school bombing, with Trump blaming Iran despite evidence of U.S. missiles hitting the site, raising questions about accountability and strategy.

item.title:
IEA ANNOUNCES 400M-BARREL RELEASE, TIMING UNCLEAR
The International Energy Agency says 400 million barrels could be released, but details on pace and timing are missing. In 2022, the U.S. peaked at about 1 million barrels/day for a month.
This may be the first time the world releases at full speed, creating supply rates the market has never seen. Analysts and traders remain uncertain how much oil will actually reach markets and how quickly.
item.description:
IEA ANNOUNCES 400M-BARREL RELEASE, TIMING UNCLEAR

The International Energy Agency says 400 million barrels could be released, but details on pace and timing are missing. In 2022, the U.S. peaked at about 1 million barrels/day for a month.

This may be the first time the world releases at full speed, creating supply rates the market has never seen. Analysts and traders remain uncertain how much oil will actually reach markets and how quickly.

item.title:
TRUMP SAYS ‘NOTHING LEFT TO TARGET’ IN IRAN WAR
Donald Trump told Axios the conflict with Iran will end “soon” because there is “practically nothing left to target.” He added, “Any time I want it to end, it will end.”
U.S. and Israeli officials, however, plan at least two more weeks of strikes. CENTCOM reports Iranian drone and missile attacks have dropped, while U.S. strikes destroyed 16 mine-laying boats in the Strait of Hormuz. Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz says operations will continue until all objectives are met.
item.description:
TRUMP SAYS ‘NOTHING LEFT TO TARGET’ IN IRAN WAR

Donald Trump told Axios the conflict with Iran will end “soon” because there is “practically nothing left to target.” He added, “Any time I want it to end, it will end.”

U.S. and Israeli officials, however, plan at least two more weeks of strikes. CENTCOM reports Iranian drone and missile attacks have dropped, while U.S. strikes destroyed 16 mine-laying boats in the Strait of Hormuz. Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz says operations will continue until all objectives are met.

item.title:
GOLDMAN WARNS OF POTENTIAL ‘EXTREME’ STOCK RALLY
Goldman Sachs’ John Flood says hedge fund positioning could fuel a sharp stock surge if good news emerges. Speculative bulls have high gross exposure (307%) while heavily shorting ETFs and index futures, creating right-tail upside risk.
A ceasefire in the Iran conflict or positive headlines could trigger 2–3% index gains as macro shorts unwind. Market volatility remains high due to war, AI, credit fears, and thin liquidity, while corporate buybacks support equities. Flood notes stocks need a resolution signal soon or risk further stress.
item.description:
GOLDMAN WARNS OF POTENTIAL ‘EXTREME’ STOCK RALLY

Goldman Sachs’ John Flood says hedge fund positioning could fuel a sharp stock surge if good news emerges. Speculative bulls have high gross exposure (307%) while heavily shorting ETFs and index futures, creating right-tail upside risk.

A ceasefire in the Iran conflict or positive headlines could trigger 2–3% index gains as macro shorts unwind. Market volatility remains high due to war, AI, credit fears, and thin liquidity, while corporate buybacks support equities. Flood notes stocks need a resolution signal soon or risk further stress.
